description:
You are a container-platform specialist who maintains a small in-house microservice that lives in
/home/user/microservice.

At the moment the directory already contains two files:

1. /home/user/microservice/version.txt  
   • single line, exactly the string “1.2.4” (no surrounding spaces, ends with a Unix newline)

2. /home/user/microservice/CHANGELOG.md  
   ```
   # Changelog

   ## [1.2.4] - 2023-01-15
   ### Added
   - Initial release.
   ```

Your job is to perform a semantic-version *minor* bump (1.2.4 ➜ 1.3.0) and record the change.

Required end-state of the system
================================

A. /home/user/microservice/version.txt  
   • must now contain one line only:  
     1.3.0

B. /home/user/microservice/CHANGELOG.md  
   • must still start with the header “# Changelog”  
   • A new entry must be inserted directly underneath that header and a blank line, **before** the existing 1.2.4 section.  
   • The new entry **must exactly follow** this template (brackets, headings and spacing are significant and will be checked by the test suite):

   ```
   ## [1.3.0] - YYYY-MM-DD
   ### Changed
   - Bumped minor version from 1.2.4 to 1.3.0 for container optimizations.
   ```

   where `YYYY-MM-DD` is the current system date at time of execution as produced by  
   `date +%Y-%m-%d`.

   The finished file will therefore look like:

   ```
   # Changelog

   ## [1.3.0] - YYYY-MM-DD
   ### Changed
   - Bumped minor version from 1.2.4 to 1.3.0 for container optimizations.

   ## [1.2.4] - 2023-01-15
   ### Added
   - Initial release.
   ```

C. /home/user/microservice/bump.log (new file)  
   • one-line log entry in **ISO-8601** date-time format:

   ```
   Bump performed on YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS from 1.2.4 to 1.3.0 by automation.
   ```

   where `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS` is the exact timestamp of execution produced by  
   `date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S`.

Operational constraints
-----------------------
• Accomplish everything using normal user privileges.  
• Only simple shell utilities (e.g., echo, printf, sed, date, etc.) are required; no external libraries or frameworks need to be installed.  
• The whole task can be completed in three to four commands.  
• Be sure the files end with a newline character and contain no extra blank spaces.  

When you are done, the CI test harness will:

1. Verify that /home/user/microservice/version.txt reads exactly “1.3.0”.
2. Parse /home/user/microservice/CHANGELOG.md to ensure the new 1.3.0 section exists, is at the top, and follows the specified text layout and current date.
3. Confirm that /home/user/microservice/bump.log exists and exactly matches the required pattern with a valid ISO-8601 timestamp.

No other changes to the filesystem are allowed.
truth:
Initial state (before agent starts)
-----------------------------------
Directory: /home/user/microservice/
  ├── version.txt         → contains single line: “1.2.4\n”
  └── CHANGELOG.md        → contains exactly:
       "# Changelog\n\n## [1.2.4] - 2023-01-15\n### Added\n- Initial release.\n"

Target (verification) state
---------------------------
1. /home/user/microservice/version.txt  
   → “1.3.0\n”

2. /home/user/microservice/CHANGELOG.md  
   → must equal the concatenation of:
      "# Changelog\n\n" +
      "## [1.3.0] - $(date +%Y-%m-%d)\n" +
      "### Changed\n" +
      "- Bumped minor version from 1.2.4 to 1.3.0 for container optimizations.\n\n" +
      "## [1.2.4] - 2023-01-15\n" +
      "### Added\n" +
      "- Initial release.\n"

3. /home/user/microservice/bump.log  
   → single line matching the regex:  
     ^Bump performed on [0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2} from 1\.2\.4 to 1\.3\.0 by automation\.\n?$

The harness will calculate the current date/time at runtime and compare.

Permissions
-----------
All files are within /home/user/microservice and are owned by the normal user, writable without elevated privileges.
